title: Baby batch 2: WIN-014 citation verification — Sun et al. 2010 and Xu et al. ~2010 lookup results
target: data/wins.json [014].detail_evidence — citation verification for Sun et al. 2010 (Chinese SG null) and Xu et al. ~2010 (2009 ΔB measurements)
search_log: CrossRef query: query.author=Heping+Sun query=eclipse+2009 — no eclipse-gravity paper found; found Xu+Sun atmospheric loading paper (2009), FCN variation paper (2009)

CrossRef query: superconducting+gravimeter+solar+eclipse+2009+July+China+gravity 2009-2012 — found New Scientist July 2009 eclipse gravity hunt article (DOI 10.1016/s0262-4079(09)61934-5); no Sun/Xu SG-null paper
CrossRef query: query.bibliographic=Sun+eclipse+gravity+2010+China+superconducting — found extensive Heping Sun SG corpus (Wuhan IGG/CAS), none specifically about 2009 eclipse results

findings.ISS-1973: Sun Hong-Ping is confirmed as a real researcher. Romanization evidence: CrossRef indexes this author as He-Ping Sun / Heping Sun / H.-P. Sun (same person: Wuhan Institute of Geodesy and Geophysics, Chinese Academy of Sciences). Extensive SG corpus including tidal gravity, FCN, Sumatra oscillations, Lhasa OSG-057. Frequent coauthor: Jianqiao Xu. No English-language paper specifically about the 2009 July 22 eclipse SG results was found in CrossRef. The 2009 eclipse DID generate English-language documentation of a gravity-anomaly hunt (New Scientist, DOI 10.1016/s0262-4079(09)61934-5). The Sun et al. 2010 paper attributed by the dome is likely published in Chinese-language Chinese Journal of Geophysics (Acta Geophysica Sinica) or similar Chinese-language journal not indexed in CrossRef. WIN-014 already acknowledges this: closest verified peer-reviewed record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013. No text change needed for this ISS — the current attribution is already honest about verification status.
findings.ISS-1972: No specific Xu et al. ~2010 paper providing station-level 2009 eclipse ΔB measurements from Hangzhou/HZT, Changchun/CHC, or Beijing/BMT was found in English-language CrossRef. Jianqiao Xu is confirmed as a Wuhan IGG coauthor (multiple Sun-Xu papers found). A 2009 Chinese-language paper is plausible but not independently verified. The consequence noted in ISS-1972 is valid: the current WIN-014 detail_evidence infers 2009 East Asian eclipse ΔB from the global ensemble (Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 39-eclipse ensemble) rather than a specific 2009 Chinese-station measurement. This is an argument weakness that should be acknowledged with a qualifier.

patches.old_string: Eclipse-magnetic ensemble studies of total eclipses (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — the same 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements referenced by WIN-068) find typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories.
patches.new_string: Eclipse-magnetic ensemble studies of total eclipses (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — the same 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements referenced by WIN-068) find typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories. Note: this ΔB estimate derives from a global ensemble (5–15 nT range across 207 measurements); specific 2009 Chinese-station ΔB readings from Hangzhou, Changchun, or Beijing magnetometers would provide a more direct citation for the 2009 event but have not been independently located in English-language literature.
patches.description: ISS-1972: add honest qualifier noting the 2009 ΔB estimate is from global ensemble inference, not a directly-cited 2009 Chinese-station measurement. Appropriate epistemic transparency without weakening the core argument (ensemble 5–15 nT range is well-established; the qualifier is methodological honesty).

obe_resolution_details.ISS-1973: No English-language DOI found for Sun et al. 2010 specifically. Author identity verified (Heping Sun = Sun Hong-Ping, Wuhan IGG CAS). Paper likely in Chinese-language CJG not indexed in CrossRef. WIN-014 already acknowledges this honestly: closest verified peer-reviewed record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306. No text change needed — current attribution is already appropriately qualified.

tldr_evidence: Web search confirmed Sun Hong-Ping = Heping Sun at Wuhan IGG with extensive SG publication record. The 2009 eclipse generated documented gravity-hunt interest (New Scientist 2009). No English-language DOI found for Sun et al. 2010 or Xu et al. ~2010 eclipse ΔB papers. For ISS-1973: WIN-014 current attribution is already honest about verification status — OBE. For ISS-1972: the 5–15 nT ΔB estimate is ensemble-derived, not 2009-specific; adding a transparency qualifier is appropriate.
tldr_verdict: ISS-1973 resolved as OBE (no change needed, current text already appropriately qualified). ISS-1972 addressed with a one-sentence em-tagged qualifier noting the ensemble inference basis and absence of a directly-cited Chinese-station 2009 measurement.
notes: The PATCH-1972 qualifier uses em tags to visually offset the epistemic note from the substantive claim, consistent with similar caveats in other WIN detail_evidence fields. The note does not weaken the core argument — 5-15 nT is well-established from 207 measurements across 39 eclipses — it simply documents the methodological basis of the specific number. Curmudgeon should verify this is the right style for citation-transparency caveats in WIN text.
